title: Incorrect release of forced assignment
body:
Verilator version: befb415f278af51fc3ecc3dda1dfe820de023bd1
Test case:

```systemverilog
// verilator lint_off MULTIDRIVEN
module t (clk);
  input clk;

  logic [31:0] lhs1, lhs2, rhs;
  logic cond = 0;

  always_comb lhs1 = rhs;
  assign lhs2 = rhs;

  always @(posedge clk) rhs = '1;

  always @(negedge clk) begin
    if (cond) begin
      force lhs1 = 'hdeadbeef;
      force lhs2 = 'hfeedface;
    end
    else begin
      release lhs1;
      release lhs2;
    end
  end

  int cyc = 0;
  always @(posedge clk) begin
    cyc <= cyc + 1;
    if (cyc == 0) cond <= 1;
    if (cyc == 3) cond <= 0;
    if (cyc > 0) begin
      $display("cyc=%0d, cond=%b, lhs1=%x, lhs2=%x", cyc, cond, lhs1, lhs2);
      if (cond) begin
        if (lhs1 != 'hdeadbeef) $stop;
        if (lhs2 != 'hfeedface) $stop;
      end
      else begin
        if (lhs1 != '1) $stop;
        if (lhs2 != '1) $stop;
      end
      if (cyc > 8) begin
        $write("*-* All Finished *-*\n");
        $finish;
      end
    end
  end
endmodule
```

Output:

```
cyc=1, cond=1, lhs1=deadbeef, lhs2=feedface
cyc=2, cond=1, lhs1=deadbeef, lhs2=feedface
cyc=3, cond=1, lhs1=deadbeef, lhs2=feedface
cyc=4, cond=0, lhs1=deadbeef, lhs2=deadbeef
%Error: test.v:36: Verilog $stop
```

`lhs2` is set to `lhs1`'s override for some reason.
With `-O0` it's a bit better:

```
cyc=1, cond=1, lhs1=deadbeef, lhs2=feedface
cyc=2, cond=1, lhs1=deadbeef, lhs2=feedface
cyc=3, cond=1, lhs1=deadbeef, lhs2=feedface
cyc=4, cond=0, lhs1=deadbeef, lhs2=ffffffff
%Error: test.v:36: Verilog $stop
```

What's interesting, if we swap the `lhs1` and `lhs2` declarations, we get a different result.

```systemverilog
  logic [31:0] lhs2, lhs1, rhs;
```

Without `-O0` it actually works:

```
cyc=1, cond=1, lhs1=deadbeef, lhs2=feedface
cyc=2, cond=1, lhs1=deadbeef, lhs2=feedface
cyc=3, cond=1, lhs1=deadbeef, lhs2=feedface
cyc=4, cond=0, lhs1=ffffffff, lhs2=ffffffff
cyc=5, cond=0, lhs1=ffffffff, lhs2=ffffffff
cyc=6, cond=0, lhs1=ffffffff, lhs2=ffffffff
cyc=7, cond=0, lhs1=ffffffff, lhs2=ffffffff
cyc=8, cond=0, lhs1=ffffffff, lhs2=ffffffff
cyc=9, cond=0, lhs1=ffffffff, lhs2=ffffffff
*-* All Finished *-*
- test.v:41: Verilog $finish
```

With `-O0` it's the same as the original code:

```
cyc=1, cond=1, lhs1=deadbeef, lhs2=feedface
cyc=2, cond=1, lhs1=deadbeef, lhs2=feedface
cyc=3, cond=1, lhs1=deadbeef, lhs2=feedface
cyc=4, cond=0, lhs1=deadbeef, lhs2=ffffffff
%Error: test.v:36: Verilog $stop
```

Not sure what's going on here, looks to me like two bugs on top of one another (invalid release + misoptimization).
